document_name: Kalihi Palama Health Center - Combined Policy Suite
posture_summary: Kalihi Palama Health Center exhibits critical compliance gaps across HIPAA, HITECH, and NIST SP 800-53 controls, with no breach notification procedures, no risk analysis, no business associate agreements, and no contingency or disaster recovery plans documented. The organization's incident response documents address operational steps for individual scenarios but lack the foundational compliance framework required of a covered entity, creating substantial regulatory exposure including potential civil money penalties. Immediate remediation is required across notification obligations, risk management, workforce training, and contingency planning to achieve a baseline compliant posture.

gaps.requirement: Covered entities must notify individuals, HHS, and media of breaches as defined under HITECH.
gaps.finding: HITECH breach notification obligations are entirely absent; only FBI and insurance are notified.
gaps.gap_description: HITECH breach notification obligations are entirely absent; only FBI and insurance are notified.

gaps.requirement: Business associates must comply with HIPAA Security Rule safeguards under HITECH Section 13401.
gaps.finding: Entity type is unknown; no BAA references, security safeguard documentation, or HITECH compliance program is present.
gaps.gap_description: Entity type is unknown; no BAA references, security safeguard documentation, or HITECH compliance program is present.
_sources: Sentinel One (endpoint protection) meltdown protocol; Bad update.pdf

gaps.requirement: Notify affected individuals, HHS, and media (if applicable) following discovery of a breach of unsecured ePHI.
gaps.finding: No breach notification policy, procedure, timeline, or responsible party is identified anywhere in the document.
gaps.gap_description: No breach notification policy, procedure, timeline, or responsible party is identified anywhere in the document.
_sources: Sentinel One (endpoint protection) meltdown protocol; Bad update.pdf

gaps.control_name: Temporary Breach Notification Requirement for Vendors
gaps.requirement: Business associates must notify covered entities of breaches without unreasonable delay and within 60 days.
gaps.evidence: Section 3: 'Official letter from the vendor' listed as a credible claim trigger for escalation.
gaps.finding: Vendor notification is acknowledged as a trigger but no formal BA notification agreement, timeline, or protocol is defined.
gaps.gap_description: Vendor notification is acknowledged as a trigger but no formal BA notification agreement, timeline, or protocol is defined.

gaps.requirement: HHS must conduct periodic audits of covered entities and business associates for HIPAA compliance.
gaps.evidence: Section 7: annual plan review and tabletop exercises mentioned; section 6 references adherence to applicable regulations.
gaps.finding: Annual review and testing referenced but no OCR audit readiness, documentation retention, or audit response procedures defined.
gaps.gap_description: Annual review and testing referenced but no OCR audit readiness, documentation retention, or audit response procedures defined.

gaps.requirement: State attorneys general may bring civil actions for HIPAA violations affecting state residents.
gaps.evidence: Section 5.2: legal counsel engagement and law enforcement notification referenced; section 6 cites regulatory compliance intent.
gaps.finding: Legal counsel role and regulatory compliance commitment present but no state AG enforcement awareness or state-specific obligations defined.
gaps.gap_description: Legal counsel role and regulatory compliance commitment present but no state AG enforcement awareness or state-specific obligations defined.

gaps.requirement: Develop, document, disseminate, review, and update incident response policy and procedures
gaps.evidence: Document provides step-by-step response procedure for a SentinelOne BSOD scenario including containment, fix, and lessons learned notes.
gaps.finding: A rudimentary procedure exists for this specific scenario, partially satisfying the procedural element, though not a formal reviewed policy.
gaps.gap_description: A rudimentary procedure exists for this specific scenario, partially satisfying the procedural element, though not a formal reviewed policy.
_sources: Sentinel One (endpoint protection) meltdown protocol; Bad update.pdf

gaps.requirement: Implement IR capability including preparation, detection, analysis, containment, eradication, and recovery.
gaps.evidence: Sections 4.1-4.5 cover Preparation, Identification, Containment, Eradication, and Recovery phases with specific steps.
gaps.finding: All six core IR handling phases are addressed with named personnel, tools (SentinelOne), and procedures.
gaps.gap_description: All six core IR handling phases are addressed with named personnel, tools (SentinelOne), and procedures.
_sources: Sentinel One (endpoint protection) meltdown protocol; Bad update.pdf

gaps.requirement: Track and document incidents on an ongoing basis.
gaps.evidence: Section 4.2: 'Log in Google docs each action taken, Date, approx time, person who executed it, what was noticed.'
gaps.finding: Ongoing incident logging documented with required data fields; Google Docs designated as the logging platform.
gaps.gap_description: Ongoing incident logging documented with required data fields; Google Docs designated as the logging platform.

gaps.gap_description: External law enforcement and government reporting contacts are present. No timeframes for reporting are defined.

gaps.finding: No change management or change control process defined. Updates are blocked reactively with no formal approval workflow.
gaps.gap_description: No change management or change control process defined. Updates are blocked reactively with no formal approval workflow.
_sources: Sentinel One (endpoint protection) meltdown protocol; Bad update.pdf

gaps.requirement: Establish and document configuration settings for IT products and enforce them
gaps.finding: No baseline configuration standards referenced. Systems identified as running old Windows versions with no remediation timeline.
gaps.gap_description: No baseline configuration standards referenced. Systems identified as running old Windows versions with no remediation timeline.
_sources: Sentinel One (endpoint protection) meltdown protocol; Bad update.pdf

gaps.requirement: HHS may audit covered entities and BAs for HIPAA compliance; organizations must maintain required documentation
gaps.finding: No audit readiness, documentation retention, or policy review process is present. Document would not meet OCR audit protocol requirements.
gaps.gap_description: No audit readiness, documentation retention, or policy review process is present. Document would not meet OCR audit protocol requirements.
_sources: Sentinel One (endpoint protection) meltdown protocol; Bad update.pdf

gaps.requirement: Employ spam protection mechanisms at information system entry and exit points.
gaps.evidence: Mimecast trace and remediation; add email address or domain to company block list; alert vendor (Proofpoint); Mimecast Recall email feature.
gaps.finding: Mimecast and Proofpoint are deployed for spam filtering. Blocklisting and email recall procedures are documented.
gaps.gap_description: Mimecast and Proofpoint are deployed for spam filtering. Blocklisting and email recall procedures are documented.

gaps.requirement: Identify events to be logged and coordinate the event logging function.
gaps.finding: No event logging policy or list of auditable events is defined. SysAid ticket logging is mentioned but is not an audit logging control.
gaps.gap_description: No event logging policy or list of auditable events is defined. SysAid ticket logging is mentioned but is not an audit logging control.

gaps.requirement: Establish and document usage restrictions and implementation guidance for remote access.
gaps.finding: Remote access controls are not addressed in this document. No remote access policy or restrictions are defined.
gaps.gap_description: Remote access controls are not addressed in this document. No remote access policy or restrictions are defined.

gaps.requirement: Provide security awareness training to all users as part of initial and ongoing training.
gaps.finding: No security awareness training program is described. KnowBe4 is used as a reporting button, but no training curriculum or schedule is mentioned.
gaps.gap_description: No security awareness training program is described. KnowBe4 is used as a reporting button, but no training curriculum or schedule is mentioned.

gaps.requirement: Implement mechanism to encrypt and decrypt ePHI where appropriate.
gaps.finding: No encryption controls are referenced for email containing ePHI. The document does not address email encryption at rest or in transit.
gaps.gap_description: No encryption controls are referenced for email containing ePHI. The document does not address email encryption at rest or in transit.

gaps.requirement: Define what constitutes a breach of unsecured PHI requiring notification under the Breach Notification Rule.
gaps.finding: No breach definition or notification trigger is present. Document does not address whether email incidents involving PHI constitute a reportable breach.
gaps.gap_description: No breach definition or notification trigger is present. Document does not address whether email incidents involving PHI constitute a reportable breach.

gaps.requirement: Enforce limit of consecutive invalid logon attempts; lock account for defined period after limit exceeded.
gaps.finding: No lockout policy, thresholds, or automatic account locking mechanism is described in the document.
gaps.gap_description: No lockout policy, thresholds, or automatic account locking mechanism is described in the document.

gaps.requirement: Manage system authenticators including password complexity, change frequency, and revocation.
gaps.evidence: Semi-annual email reminders to change passwords and keep them unique; 1 to 1 intervention to make sure account credentials are changed.
gaps.finding: Password change reminders and uniqueness mentioned but no complexity, minimum length, history, or MFA requirements are defined.
gaps.gap_description: Password change reminders and uniqueness mentioned but no complexity, minimum length, history, or MFA requirements are defined.

gaps.requirement: Develop, document, and periodically update a security and privacy plan that describes controls in place.
gaps.finding: No system security plan, scope statement, ownership, or review schedule exists in this document.
gaps.gap_description: No system security plan, scope statement, ownership, or review schedule exists in this document.

gaps.requirement: Implement procedures to verify that a person or entity seeking access to ePHI is the one claimed.
gaps.finding: No authentication controls, MFA, or identity verification procedures for ePHI system access are mentioned.
gaps.gap_description: No authentication controls, MFA, or identity verification procedures for ePHI system access are mentioned.

gaps.control_name: HITECH Security Rule Application to Business Associates
gaps.requirement: Business associates must comply with HIPAA Security Rule requirements directly under HITECH.
gaps.finding: No BA agreements, BA security obligations, or downstream compliance requirements are addressed.
gaps.gap_description: No BA agreements, BA security obligations, or downstream compliance requirements are addressed.

gaps.control_name: HITECH Access to PHI - Restrictions on Sale
gaps.requirement: Covered entities and business associates must not sell PHI without valid authorization except in permitted circumstances.
gaps.finding: No PHI use restrictions, sale prohibitions, or data handling obligations are described in the document.
gaps.gap_description: No PHI use restrictions, sale prohibitions, or data handling obligations are described in the document.

gaps.requirement: Notify prominent media outlets for breaches affecting more than 500 residents of a state or jurisdiction.
gaps.finding: No media notification requirement or threshold assessment is mentioned in the document.
gaps.gap_description: No media notification requirement or threshold assessment is mentioned in the document.

items.title: Develop HIPAA-HITECH Breach Notification Policy
items.description: Draft and adopt a breach notification policy covering 60-day individual notice, HHS Secretary reporting via OCR portal, and media notification for breaches over 500 individuals.

items.title: Conduct Formal HIPAA Security Risk Analysis
items.description: Engage qualified personnel or a third party to perform a documented risk analysis identifying threats, vulnerabilities, likelihood, and impact to all ePHI systems.

items.title: Establish Risk Management Program
items.description: Implement a written risk management plan documenting security measures selected to reduce ePHI risks to reasonable and appropriate levels, tied to risk analysis findings.

items.title: Execute Business Associate Agreements
items.description: Identify all vendors and contractors handling ePHI including the IT consultant, execute HIPAA-compliant BAAs, and document BA breach notification obligations within 60 days.

items.title: Create Breach Log and Annual HHS Reporting Process
items.description: Establish a breach tracking log for incidents affecting fewer than 500 individuals and define the annual HHS reporting workflow including responsible owner and submission timeline.

phases.name: Phase 2 - Contingency Planning and Access Controls

items.title: Develop HIPAA Contingency Plan Suite
items.description: Create documented data backup plan, disaster recovery plan, emergency mode operation plan, and contingency testing procedures covering all ePHI systems per 164.308(a)(7).

items.title: Define Backup, Recovery, and Reconstitution Procedures
items.description: Document backup frequency, offsite storage, retention periods, test restoration schedule, and formal return-to-operations criteria for all ePHI systems.

items.title: Implement ePHI Access Control Policy
items.description: Define role-based access controls, minimum necessary standard, unique user identification, emergency access procedures, and credential lifecycle management for all ePHI systems.

items.title: Establish Formal Audit Control and Log Review Procedures
items.description: Define audit logging requirements for all ePHI systems, establish review frequency and escalation chain, and set minimum 6-year documentation retention policy.

items.title: Update Incident Response Plan with Compliance Requirements
items.description: Revise IRP to add ePHI identification steps, response metrics, plan protection classification, resource requirements, and integration with the contingency plan.

phases.name: Phase 3 - Workforce, Training, and Detection Capabilities

items.title: Launch Role-Based HIPAA and IR Training Program
items.description: Develop and deliver HIPAA workforce training and role-specific IR training, document completion, and define recurring frequency and refresher requirements for all staff.

items.title: Define Malicious Software and Phishing Reporting Procedures
items.description: Create user-facing procedures for reporting suspicious email, malware, and phishing attempts, integrating with MimeCast and SentinelOne workflows and escalation path.

items.title: Establish Vulnerability Management and Patch Program
items.description: Define vulnerability scanning schedule, tooling, remediation SLAs, and patch management process covering all ePHI-adjacent systems and endpoints.

items.title: Implement Security Alerts and Advisory Intake Process
items.description: Subscribe to HHS, US-CERT, and vendor security advisories and create a documented process for triaging, disseminating, and acting on external threat intelligence.

items.title: Conduct Incident Response and Contingency Tabletop Exercises
items.description: Schedule and execute tabletop exercises for breach, ransomware, and power outage scenarios with defined frequency, success criteria, and after-action documentation requirements.

phases.name: Phase 4 - Program Maturity and Ongoing Compliance

items.title: Develop OCR Audit Readiness and Documentation Retention Plan
items.description: Compile required HIPAA policy documentation, establish 6-year retention schedule, assign document owner, and create an OCR audit response playbook.

items.title: Establish State AG Enforcement Awareness Program
items.description: Brief legal counsel on Hawaii state attorney general HIPAA enforcement authority, document state-specific obligations, and integrate into the legal response section of the IRP.

items.title: Implement Insider Threat Program
items.description: Develop written insider threat policy covering detection indicators, HR integration, monitoring procedures, sanction policy, and incident escalation path for workforce-related threats.

items.title: Formalize BA Notification Protocol and 13407 Compliance
items.description: Define contractual BA notification timelines, designate receiving contact, create intake procedures for vendor-reported breaches, and confirm 60-day notification SLA in all BAAs.

items.title: Define Encryption Policy and PHI Safe Harbor Strategy
items.description: Adopt an encryption standard for ePHI at rest and in transit, document safe harbor eligibility criteria, and integrate data classification into breach risk assessment procedures.

_doc_names: Sentinel One (endpoint protection) meltdown protocol; Bad update.pdf
